title: rubus occidentalis subsp. eriocarpus
intro: Rubus occidentalis subsp. eriocarpus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family and is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of thimbleberry include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as providing relief from diarrhea and other digestive issues. Thimbleberry can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for its medicinal uses, and it is also used in jams and preserves for its sweet, tart flavor.
